title: Mission 001: Mars Water Evidence Explorer
subtitle: A NASA-based reference demo for Lit-gated interactive learning content.
sourceNote: Reference demo uses NASA-style public-domain space science material. Replace with verified NASA API assets before external submission.

items.title: Your Mission
items.body: Look at a Mars terrain image and identify surface shapes that could suggest past fluid movement. Your goal is not to prove water existed, but to learn how scientists reason from visual evidence.

items.title: First Observation Prompt
items.body: Find three shapes in the terrain: branching channels, curved ridges, or fan-like deposits. Write what you see before reading any clues.

items.title: Clue 1 — Branching Patterns
items.body: Branching channels can be consistent with past flowing liquid, but similar shapes can also form through other geological processes. Look for repeated branching, directionality, and relation to lower terrain.

items.title: Clue 2 — Fan-Shaped Deposits
items.body: A fan-shaped deposit near the end of a channel can suggest transported sediment. Ask: where would material have come from, and where would it settle?

items.title: Answer Key Guidance
items.body: Students should distinguish observation from conclusion. Accept answers that cite visible evidence and uncertainty. Do not reward claims that a single image proves liquid water.

items.title: Common Misconception
items.body: A water-like shape is not direct proof of water. The learning objective is evidence ranking: shape, context, alternative explanations, and confidence.

items.title: Source Mapping Note
items.body: Before external release, connect this module to a specific NASA API image, citation, mission page, and a short source note explaining why the image is suitable for this lesson.

items.title: Content Design Rationale
items.body: The module separates public observation, learner clues, teacher answers, and source annotations to demonstrate progressive access rather than static content locking.

student_hint.description: Generate a hint after the learner attempts the observation.

teacher_script.description: Prepare teacher guidance and answer discussion.

source_review.description: Review source annotations and content-design rationale.

media.title: A Complex Valley Network Near Idaeus Fossae
media.description: Many valleys occur all over Mars that reveal an extensive ancient history of liquid water erosion. This image from NASA Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter shows a complex valley network near Idaeus Fossae.
